programme.title: Canada Tonight
programme.desc: Canada Tonight was a Canadian television newscast which aired on stations owned by Western International Communications from 1993 to 2001. It was produced out of the studio of CHAN-TV in Burnaby, British Columbia. There were two versions of the newscast; the one seen outside BC was anchored by Tony Parsons, and the one seen in that province was anchored by Bill Good.

programme.desc: No one shows up for Loris birthday, so the Stanleys try to cheer her up by recounting their worst birthday stories.

programme.title: Driving Me Crazy
programme.desc: A self-proclaimed ladies man seeks hot wheels to impress the opposite sex. Also, overconfidence leads to a tough day for a father.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series
programme.desc: Mavis werewolf pals from her old Ghoul Guide troop are coming to the hotel for a visit and Mavis is super excited because its their chance to finally earn the last badge on their Ghoul Guides sash; Frankenstein wants to try his hand at serious, dramatic acting, so he can win a shiny award.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series
programme.desc: Its Mavis Fangceanera and shes turning 115. Not only is it a party, but a vampire rite of passage as she finally earns her Dracula cape.

programme.desc: The school is up for grabs in a climbing contest between Le Chef and Miss Mush; Dana tries to maintain her perfect attendance record while helping her friends.

programme.desc: Miss Mush foretells that Maurecia will be a milkmaid when she grows up; Todd ignores the love note Maurecia passed to him.

programme.title: Under Wraps
programme.desc: Three 12-year-old friends happen upon and awaken a mummy, which they affectionately name Harold. Together, they must rush to return him to his resting place before midnight on Halloween. Along the way, the team narrowly escapes a nefarious group of criminals who are determined to sell the mummy to the highest bidder. When Harold is inevitably captured, Marshall, Gilbert and Amy must band together to stand up to the criminals, face their fears and rescue their new?but rather "ancient"?friend.

programme.title: Stanley Cup Final
programme.Sub-title: Game 3: Florida Panthers at Edmonton Oilers
programme.desc: Florida Panthers at Edmonton Oilers in Game 3 of the NHLs championship series. From Rogers Place. The best-of-seven heads north of the border with the Panthers up 2-0 on Edmonton after a 4-1 win sparked by two third-period goals by Evan Rodrigues. The 30-year-old center and Toronto native was born one month after the last Cup title by a Canadian team.

programme.Sub-title: Game 3: Florida Panthers at Edmonton Oilers
programme.desc: Florida Panthers at Edmonton Oilers in Game 3 of the NHLs championship series. From Rogers Place. The best-of-seven heads north of the border with the Panthers up 2-0 on Edmonton after a 4-1 win sparked by two third-period goals by Evan Rodrigues. programme.title: Chip and Potato

programme.title: Lucas the Spider

programme.title: UEFA 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: From Allianz Arena  Bavaria, DEU Germany is up against Scotland in Munich in the opening game of the 2024 European Championships. programme.title: UEFA 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: From Rhein Energie Stadium  Cologne, DEU Hungary is up against Switzerland in Cologne in Group A of the 2024 European Championships. The only major tournament meeting was in the 1938 World Cup quarter-finals. Hungary won 2-0 in Lille courtesy of goals by György Sárosi and Gyula Zsengellér.

programme.desc: The latest news and analyses from the UEFA European Championship.

programme.title: UEFA 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: From Olympiastadion  Berlin, DEU Spain takes on Croatia in Berlin in Group B of the 2024 European Championships. These nations have been drawn together for the fourth successive EUROs. This includes Spain winning 5-3 after extra time in Copenhagen in the EURO 2020 round of last 16.

programme.title: Euro Today
programme.desc: The latest news and analyses from the UEFA European Championship.

programme.title: UEFA 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: From Signal Iduna Park  North Rhine-Westphalia, DEU Italy plays Albania in Dortmund in Group B of the 2024 European Championships. Italy has won all four matches against Albania. The most recent of those games was a 3-1 friendly victory in Tirana on Nov. 16, 2022 (including two Vincenzo Grifo goals).

programme.title: UEFA 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: From Allianz Arena  Bavaria, DEU Germany is up against Scotland in Munich in the opening game of the 2024 European Championships. programme.title: UEFA 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: From Rhein Energie Stadium  Cologne, DEU Hungary is up against Switzerland in Cologne in Group A of the 2024 European Championships. The only major tournament meeting was in the 1938 World Cup quarter-finals. Hungary won 2-0 in Lille courtesy of goals by György Sárosi and Gyula Zsengellér.

programme.title: UEFA 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: From Olympiastadion  Berlin, DEU Spain takes on Croatia in Berlin in Group B of the 2024 European Championships. These nations have been drawn together for the fourth successive EUROs. This includes Spain winning 5-3 after extra time in Copenhagen in the EURO 2020 round of last 16.

programme.title: Euro Today
programme.desc: The latest news and analyses from the UEFA European Championship.

programme.title: UEFA 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: From Signal Iduna Park  North Rhine-Westphalia, DEU Italy plays Albania in Dortmund in Group B of the 2024 European Championships. Italy has won all four matches against Albania. The most recent of those games was a 3-1 friendly victory in Tirana on Nov. 16, 2022 (including two Vincenzo Grifo goals).

programme.title: NBA 2024 NBA Finals
programme.desc: From American Airlines Center  Dallas, TX, USA The Celtics try to take a 3-0 lead over the Mavericks in Game 3 of the NBA Finals. Boston, vying for a record 18th title, prevailed 105-98 in Game 2 behind 26 points and 11 rebounds by Jrue Holiday. The Celtics are 6-0 on the road this postseason.

programme.title: Big Food Bucket List
programme.desc: Johns en route to Calgary, Alberta for ramen so amazing people line up all day; a super-stacked pizza with an amazing crust in Kamloops, British Columbia; a perfect cheeseburger in Toronto.

programme.Sub-title: Taco Walk on the Wild Side
programme.desc: John gets a first-hand lesson in charcoal grilling and checks ultra-juicy, braised lamb barbacoa tacos off his bucket list; John gets a lesson in fresh noodle making and a taste of the best pork wontons; a mile-high, double-thick pork chop.

programme.title: Big Food Bucket List
programme.desc: John heads to Toronto to count down the 100 layers on a sky-high, pan-fried lasagna; he treks to Chicago to meet the baron of Midwestern BBQ and dig his fork into an award-winning brisket; and a brunch bowl chock full of West African flavors.

programme.title: Project Bakeover
programme.desc: Steve is in New Brunswick to ease the leadership transition from Bakery Owner Mike to his daughter Danika; he tries to give this family business a new lease on life with a new menu and new décor.

programme.title: Project Bakeover
programme.desc: New Brunswick bakery owner Jonathan is a big kid at heart, but theres no time to monkey around when his shops success is on the line; over the course of just one week, Steve creates a new menu in an attempt to get Monkey Cakes back on track.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Spatchcock chicken sheet pan supper; layered loaf pan lasagna; frontier-style steak frites and oven fries with lemon and pepper seasoning and easy Hollandaise.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Chicken fried steak sliders piled on warm buttermilk biscuits and served with buttermilk gravy; perfect fried potatoes; cherry pie cookie bars.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Breakfast tostada topped with fried egg; pineapple chicken tacos; a bowl of pozole with plenty of fixings; quick churros with caramel sauce.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond honors her favorite mothers by making sweet deliveries; she delivers strawberry cheesecake brownies, a blood orange rosemary pound cake, a fruit bouquet, edible kisses, and vanilla meringue cookies.

programme.title: The Grill Dads
programme.desc: Mark and Ryan visit La Waffleria in Albuquerque, N.M., where they tackle a ranchero waffle; they sample a sugary doughnut breakfast sandwich at Straw in San Francisco; the Dads visit Denver in search of a biscuit transformed into French toast.

programme.title: The Grill Dads
programme.desc: Mark and Ryan eat cheesy nachos piled high with elk meat and drink floats made with stout beer; the baked potato is re-imagined as bite-sized gnocchi; Tilt serves up a teriyaki island trucker burger; a peanut butter pie is turned into a milkshake.

programme.title: The Grill Dads
programme.desc: Classic chicken pot pie topped with a homemade, buttery puff pastry dough; a plate of homemade beef chili served underneath a decadent mac and cheese; the Dads travel to the heart of barbecue country -- Kansas City, Mo.

programme.title: The Grill Dads
programme.desc: The Grill Dads head to Jackson, Wyo., for an enormous 40-ounce steak served alongside crunchy rattlesnake jalapeno poppers; house-made pork belly into a spectacular BLT sandwich; green chorizo served on top of black beans with cumin creme fraiche.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and Que
programme.desc: Michael Symon and the winner of "Worst Cooks in America: Celebrity Edition," Jenni "JWoww" Farley, team up to tackle classic barbecue, fresh farmhouse burgers and microbrews; one of the areas best-known chefs gives his take on the local cuisine.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and Que
programme.Sub-title: Beers, Burgers and Bocce in Baltimore
programme.desc: Josh Charles meets up with Michael at his favorite place for crab cakes in town; after a round of bocce ball, Michael introduces Josh to some of Baltimores best new spots for juicy, inventive burgers and craft brews.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and Que
programme.desc: Michael Symon gets in touch with his Greek heritage with a classic lamb and feta cheeseburger; an Italian-style burger stuffed with smoky mozzarella and prosciutto; a rabbit-meat hot dog with chili pepper toppings so spicy theres a release waiver.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and Que
programme.desc: Michael Symon heads to Asheville, N.C., where he smokes a whole hog in traditional Carolina style for the juiciest pulled pork sandwich; beignets stuffed with crawfish, shrimp and andouille sausage; burger with Atomic Death sauce and Hell Relish.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and Que
programme.desc: A Sloppy Jai with lamb hash and 30 Indian spices; towers of Japanese-American fusion with katsu-style deep-fried burger patties; pastrami-spiced burnt ends pork belly and spicy, jalapeno-glazed lamb pops fresh out of the smoker.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and Que
programme.desc: Smoking a whole hog and making chorizo from scratch for a saucy barbecue brunch burrito with two different fire-roasted salsas; Caribbean roasted pork sandwich; the breakfast burger of champions with hash browns, bacon and a fried egg.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and Que
programme.Sub-title: Smoked Smorgasbord for a Sharks Appetite
programme.desc: Cheeseburger tacos with a house-made beer cheese sauce and a Jamaican jerk fried fish taco with mango salsa and cilantro sour cream; bone-in pork chop; smoked lamb sandwich with homemade mint sauce; smoked brownies.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and Que
programme.desc: A slammin sandwich with three different cuts of pork smothered in triple-cheese sauce; a flaming hot burger stuffed with spicy jalapeno Jack cheese and covered in Buffalo sauce; a brewerys drunken chicken pot pie.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and Que
programme.desc: A whole pig on a super-sized indoor smoke pit; slow-smoked rib-eye steak sandwich complete with melted cheddar and caramelized onions; double beef patty burger that gets the royal treatment with two bacon grilled cheese sandwiches for buns.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and Que
programme.desc: Michael Symon starts in Boston with triple-processed pork belly thats cured, brined and smoked; tender braised lamb shoulder amps up a traditional breakfast hash; fresh, local cinnamon rolls as buns for an insanely sweet and savory burger.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern explores the cuisine of Sitka, Alaska, where the preparation of iconic Alaskan staples like king salmon, venison and rockfish has evolved with finesse.

programme.title: Island Of The Monsoon
programme.desc: From Cloud to Coast: Monsoon storms sweep over the tropical island of Sri Lanka, beginning a journey through the many changing faces of the islands forests.

programme.title: Tales From Zambia
programme.desc: Life and Death on the Plains: The Liuwa Plains are one of Africas last wild places - vast and remote grasslands where only the continents toughest wildlife can survive. (S1, ep 3)

programme.title: Hope For Wildlife
programme.desc: The Early Bird: Deer season means the rehab is flooded with fawns. Chelsea tries to reunite one fawn with its mother, while two police officers rescue another. (S7, ep 5)

programme.title: Wild Castles
programme.desc: Predjama: In Slovenia lies the worlds largest castle thats carved into a cave - a subterranean haven for the regions birds, boars and mountain goats. (S1, ep 3)

programme.title: Wild Pacific Rescue
programme.desc: The MMR team helps a lost sea turtle get back home. A huge sea lion is found shot with a cross bow bolt, and Dr Marty helps a fruit bat cope with old age. (S1, ep 3)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: It is moving day for young adult male orang-utan Jin, who was born at the park. But he is not so keen to leave. (S13, ep 7)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison is in the Netherlands to collect Pichihua, a lonely female woolly monkey whos lost her mate and needs companionship. (S13, ep 8)

programme.title: Hope For Wildlife
programme.desc: Catch and Release: A fishermans illegal catch provides a feast for Hopes patients, and an invasive species also winds up on the menu. (S7, ep 6)

programme.title: Wild Castles
programme.desc: Alhambra: A refuge for Grenadas wildlife, Alhambras fortress provides the backdrop for the daily dramas and struggles of the regions amphibians, fish and birds. (S1, ep 4)

programme.title: Carpathian Predators
programme.desc: Realm of the Bear: See the Carpathian mountains - where vast forests, steep gorges, and isolated peaks offer refuge to some of Europes most charismatic creatures. (1 of 2)

programme.title: Into The Wild: India
programme.desc: Nagaina: Queen of Kings: In the hot forest of Southern India, a female king cobra is on a mission: to find a mate, find food, and lay eggs before the monsoon arrives. (S1, ep 9)

programme.title: Secrets Of The Enchanted Forests
programme.desc: Climb into a realm of thin air and towering rock and watch mythical monkeys do battle, an aromatic tree bring together blossom, possum enjoy a moonlit tryst and more. (4 of 6)

programme.desc: The natural world is filled with colourful and quirky characters. It may look crazy, but when the aim is to survive, nothing is too outrageous.

programme.title: A Bees Diary
programme.desc: Bees are one of the most important species on the planet. Here, follow the trials and tribulations of two particular honeybees over two years from birth to death.

programme.desc: New female chimp Kalu meets big male Bryan, and Alison is pleased with how she is coping with life at the park. Capuchin boss Erico muscles in on a log pile. (S13, ep 9)

programme.desc: Alison is on a marmoset rescue mission after being contacted by the RSPCA. With assistance from the police, she finally tracks down Millie Martin. (S13, ep 10)

programme.desc: Whatever It Takes: A fawn survives a snare and a bobcat attack - can Hope save it? A baby bobcat winds up on Hopes doorstep, and an old friend returns to the rehab. (S7, ep 7)

programme.desc: The Rescue team tries to save an otter as Dr Marty battles rough conditions to help an entangled sea lion. At the Rescue Centre, a mischievous seal pup puts himself in danger. (S1, ep 2)

programme.desc: The MMR team helps a lost sea turtle get back home. A huge sea lion is found shot with a cross bow bolt, and Dr Marty helps a fruit bat cope with old age. (S1, ep 3)

programme.desc: Hollywoods Held Hostage: The Hollywood pride is in serious trouble! Having fled north to escape the Nomads, other lions move in and threaten to take over their territory. (S1, ep 5)

programme.desc: The Next Dynasty: The MK pride faces its toughest moment yet as their matriarch and best hunter Surabis life hangs in the balance after being gored by a buffalo. (S1, ep 6)

programme.title: Rewilding
programme.desc: Hidden Gem Of The North: Welcome to the Steinhuder Meer, a biodiversity hotspot in Germany, as a combination of fenlands, alder march, and dunes create an amazing landscape. (S1, ep 2)

programme.title: Rewilding
programme.desc: Andalusias Struggle: Andalusia consists of extreme habitats once severely impacted by humans. Now, many previously near-extinct animals are reclaiming this unique land. (S1, ep 3)

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Wild City
programme.desc: Hidden Wild: With the latest film-making technology, Sir David Attenborough uncovers the unseen natural treasures of Singapore and explores how the wildlife has adapted to humans. (1 of 4)

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Wild City
programme.desc: Urban Wild: Sir David Attenborough narrates a day in the animal life of Singapores urban environments, from a troop of suburban macaques to a family of otters in Marina Bay. (2 of 4)

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Wild City
programme.desc: Islands: From sandy beaches to colourful coral reefs, Singapores islands have a host of remarkable creatures, including the tiger tail seahorse and the quick-changing cuttlefish. (3 of 4)

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Running With The Pack
programme.desc: Meet the Packs: Its time to meet the packs, ready? Theres the Manzi pack in the heartlands, the Kakuli pack in the Badlands and the Stork Colony pack at the parks edge. (S1, ep 1)

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Running With The Pack
programme.desc: A Close Encounter: Feel the heat as drought ravages the park. As the Manzi pack encounter predators on their hunt for water, the Stork Colony thrive against all odds. (S1, ep 2)

programme.desc: Join a team of wildlife filmmakers in the forests of Yala National Park, as they reveal the enigmatic sloth bears life and behaviour as never seen before. (1 of 2)

programme.desc: A sick elephant calf who was found abandoned near a reserve appears to be another victim of the battle for space between humans and elephants. (2 of 2)

programme.title: Africas Hunters
programme.desc: Kings of Nsefu: Its a dangerous time for the pride. Rival males move in on the current kings, who will be pushed to their limits to protect their family and their territory. (S2, ep 3)

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Running With The Pack
programme.desc: Meet the Packs: Its time to meet the packs, ready? Theres the Manzi pack in the heartlands, the Kakuli pack in the Badlands and the Stork Colony pack at the parks edge. (S1, ep 1)

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Running With The Pack
programme.desc: A Close Encounter: Feel the heat as drought ravages the park. As the Manzi pack encounter predators on their hunt for water, the Stork Colony thrive against all odds. (S1, ep 2)

programme.title: Secrets Of The Enchanted Forests
programme.desc: Tropical: Circle the equator for a look at the diverse species that dwell in tropical forests, such as sloths, lemurs and elephants, and uncover the regions dark side. (1 of 6)

programme.title: Secrets Of The Enchanted Forests
programme.desc: Watch the seasons wax and wane and learn how trees and animals help one another survive. (2 of 6)

programme.title: Secrets Of The Enchanted Forests
programme.desc: Wade into wetlands, including an underwater forest that protects young fur seals and a mangrove sanctuary for aquatic misfits, and meet some real-life swamp monsters. (3 of 6)

programme.title: Secrets Of The Enchanted Forests
programme.desc: Climb into a realm of thin air and towering rock and watch mythical monkeys do battle, an aromatic tree bring together blossom, possum enjoy a moonlit tryst and more. (4 of 6)

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: Lost World Of The Yucatan: The rainforest meets the Caribbean in this eastern region of Mexico. Explore almost-impenetrable jungle and dive into the areas underwater caves. (S3, ep 1)

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: Wild Heart Of Sierra Gorda: One region made up of many worlds - climb from the low hill of a semi-desert into the lush green clouds of a forest teeming with wildlife. (S3, ep 2)

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: Sky To Sea: The Sonoran Desert is a forgotten, foreboding place. With its towering volcanic peaks and almost other-worldly craters, its conditions push animal life to the limit. (S3, ep 3)

programme.title: Wild Castles
programme.desc: Heidelberg: Explore the historic home of an extraordinary family of birds of prey in a look at the wildlife living in the nature reserve surrounding Germanys Heidelberg Castle. (S1, ep 1)

programme.title: Wild Castles
programme.desc: Carcassonne: Flocks of birds, families of wild boar and armies of insects thrive in and around this centuries-old fortress. And years of human inhabitants havent put them off. (S1, ep 2)

programme.title: Wild Castles
programme.desc: Predjama: In Slovenia lies the worlds largest castle thats carved into a cave - a subterranean haven for the regions birds, boars and mountain goats. (S1, ep 3)

programme.title: Surviving The Amazon
programme.desc: Jungle Killers: A jaguar stalks the river, a snake makes a killer move and a stealth tarantula waits to strike. Nothing is safe when the Amazons predators are on the prowl. (S1, ep 1)

programme.title: Surviving The Amazon
programme.desc: Jungle Love: Meet monkeys who like monogamy, a bird who rivals Casanova and a spider wholl risk his life for love. A look at how hard it is to find the one in the jungle. (S1, ep 2)

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: Deer season means the rehab is flooded with fawns. Chelsea tries to reunite one fawn with its mother, while two police officers rescue another.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: Few places are more beautiful or more forbidding than the Sonora Desert, with its windswept dunes, towering volcanic peaks and deep, other-worldly craters. In this forgotten wilderness, plants and animals deprived of water must adapt or die.

programme.title: Into the Wild India
programme.desc: Nestled on the edge of the ancient city of Jodhpur, the luxurious Temple Gardens are home to a huge troop of Hanuman langurs, who enjoy a peaceful life of bounty. Yet, the troop has a strict hierarchy.

programme.title: Secrets of the Enchanted Forests
programme.desc: Wading into wetlands, where trees and water meet to nurture new life. Diving into an underwater forest that protects young fur seals, visiting a mangrove sanctuary where a curious band of aquatic misfits hides out, and more.

programme.Sub-title: Chobe National Park: Land of Learning

programme.title: Winter Love Story
programme.desc: When author Cassies first novel isnt selling, she is paired on a book tour with author Elliot to boost Cassies sales. However, a romance starts to blossom along the way.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Roses new beau Ernie is a wonderful man, but she has to deal with the impotence of being Ernest.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy falls in lust with the man shes dating; and Blanche and Rose fall on their faces as pals to motherless girls.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: A dateless Feb 14 prompts memories of Valentines Days past, including Sophias in a Chicago garage in 1929 (it was a killer!).

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy wants quality time with Sophia---whether Sophia likes it or not; Rose and Blanche team up on a storybook, with Rose doing the text and Blanche the artwork.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Rose professes faith in Hope, who shes sure will show up to emcee the ladies-auxiliary benefit.

programme.title: Reba
programme.desc: In the series finale, Cheyenne and Van ask Reba if they can move back in with her after Van accidentally starts a fire at their new house. Also, Barbra Jean is offered a job at a TV station in Arkansas.

programme.title: Reba
programme.desc: In the series premiere, Reba and her family go to therapy to talk about the recent breakup of her marriage to dentist husband Brock, but what Reba doesnt know is that her 17-year-old daughter, Cheyenne, also has an unexpected announcement.

programme.title: Reba
programme.Sub-title: The Honeymoons Over or Now What?
programme.desc: Cheyenne and Van come home from their honeymoon in Galveston and have to deal with returning to school---married. Meanwhile, Reba adjusts the house for their new living arrangements, but not everyone is as ready to accept the newlyweds.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Fed up with the rudeness hes been encountering, Frasier gets rough with an impudent patron at the cafe.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier cant hold his frustration in check when Martin beats him at chess.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles is going to testify to the mental incompetency of a wealthy gent who Frasier deems A-OK.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: After an attractive policewoman pulls him over, Frasier goes after her in hot pursuit---but she finds Martin more arresting.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: When a priceless vase is broken at a company party, Woody takes the rap---and the praise from the host, who lauds him for taking responsibility for his actions. And Rebecca, whos reluctantly playing "serving wench", is dismayed when the two men become fast friends.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: With Carla recuperating from the birth of her twins, her daughter-in-law fills in at the bar and falls for Sam; and Rebecca is ready to flatten the comely waitress Mr Drake hired.

programme.title: Reba
programme.Sub-title: Someones at the Gyno with Reba
programme.desc: Van moves his belongings into his and Cheyennes room, and Reba and Kyra are shocked when Cheyenne accepts the mess, which includes a large neon sign, 12 pairs of sneakers, exercise equipment and a Dallas Cowboy Cheerleaders poster. Meanwhile, Jake has his first visitation with his father, and Reba makes a discovery about herself.

programme.title: Reba
programme.desc: Cheyenne has morning sickness and tries to hide it from Van; Reba learns that Jakes friend isnt allowed to sleep over because his mother disapproves of the Harts dysfunctional lifestyle.

programme.title: Reba
programme.desc: Van gets a "kidnap breakfast", a tradition that includes the drill team making the football player of the week an early morning meal. Meanwhile, Reba decides to host the Wildcat pregame dinner for the team and everything goes well until Barbra Jean discovers the potato salad.

programme.title: Reba

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: For centuries, fantastic tales of terrifying monsters have fascinated mankind. Bizarre beasts of all shapes and sizes have been portrayed in some of historys most classic stories and folklore. Examining what if these creatures were more than just works of imagination and superstition; if these stories of legendary creatures could be inspired by actual events and where does the line between fact and fiction fall; and if these famous monsters still lurk in the dark corner of the globe.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.Sub-title: The Bermuda Triangle and the Beyond
programme.desc: All over the world are forbidden triangles-mysterious regions where ships, planes, and people enter and then just disappear. They are found in Bermuda, Alaska, Japan and elsewhere. But why? Do these locations defy the laws of physics? Or is it possible that there are some places on Earth that we are simply not meant to go?

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: For centuries archaeologists and explorers have been obsessed with locating ancient cities lost to time. Some of these places are recorded in historical texts, while others are part of oral traditions and folklore. How does a once-thriving community become a lost city. And if their ruins are found, what secrets might be they reveal about human history.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: Various stories of mysterious disappearances are featured.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: What is it about rocks and stones that inspires some people to worship them. Throughout history, many have insisted that simple stones can do everything from choosing a king to causing death. A miraculous meteorite falling from the heavens. A priceless gemstone with a deadly curse. And a chunk of sandstone considered so sacred that wars have been fought to protect it.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: An examination of the prominence that gold wields over the collective consciousness.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Investigating if King Arthur was simply a thrilling figure cobbled together from the folklore of many cultures or a flesh and blood man. King Arthur. Englands first pop culture superhero, his exploits have inspired numerous blockbuster movies and television series. Without King Arthur, there wouldnt have "Middle Earth," "Harry Potter," "Game of Thrones," "Star Wars" or "Star Trek." It is impossible to gauge the impact that the Arthurian legend has had on Western culture.

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Thousands of people report looking up at the sky and witnessing unidentified flying objects. A vast majority of these strange and disturbing incidents take place in the same locations on Earth. Examining if it is possible that extraterrestrial spacecraft are repeatedly visiting Earth at so-called "UFO Hot Spots."

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: The animal kingdom is full of deadly predators, and some of them attack in extraordinary and inexplicable ways. Do some animals strike back at humans for reasons beyond normal defense and hunger? Can they have emotions like anger or a desire for revenge? Despite our technological advancement, do animals pose a greater threat to us than we realize for reasons that we cannot explain?

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: From massive asteroids and deadly bursts of radiation to microscopic alien viruses, each one of these cosmic dangers may end human civilization as we know it. Can we prepare for the inevitable calamity that may be coming our way?

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: The blue marble we call Earth remains in large part a mystery to the 8 billion people who live on it. How did Earth come to exist, and what are the secrets of its survival? How long will humans be able to call it "home"?

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Venus, our nearest planetary neighbor, is a glowing yellow orb with massive volcanoes, poisonous clouds, and a surface temperature hot enough to melt lead. Its appearance has fascinated us for centuries, but it raises questions about whether Venus is our evil twin or a warning about Earths future.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: As we aim to travel beyond the Moon to new worlds, how are we training to be human extraterrestrials? How will we survive this journey into the unknown? Will we be able to thrive when we reach our next galactic destination? And what will happen if we make contact with intelligent life?

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Discover what we could find when we reach other planets, and what dangers we may face.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Earths moon is only one of hundreds in the solar system, each of them a mysterious and often bizarre world. Examining if alien moons offer a key to exploring outer space and perhaps even finding a new home for humanity.

programme.title: The Cars That Made America

The 50s and 60s youth want speed and fun, and the Big Three battle each other to give it to them, creating the all-American muscle car.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Jake and the pups make a grisly discovery in new territory; Josh rebuilds an antique windmill to power his outpost cabin; Kidd and Harry brave raging rapids to retrieve a capsized canoe; Martha and Elli clear the road of fallen trees.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: In Montana, Jake Herak turns all his pack loose on an aggressive mountain lion and comes face to face with a black bear. Tom and Jack Oar build traditional rawhide drums as they prepare to reopen the Trading Post. In Wyoming, Josh Kirk rebuilds his windmill and puts the finishing touches on his outpost cabin to have it ready for calving season. In Idaho, Kidd and Harry Youren lead a pack train of horses deep into the Sawtooth Mountains where they establish a wilderness fishing camp.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: In Wyoming, Josh Kirk fends off dangerous bulls to protect a bison mother while she struggles through a difficult birth. In Alaska, Mike Horstman embarks on a journey to the south end of Kodiak Island to hunt for the largest bears on earth. Martha and Elli Tansy travel to the coast to harvest clams and explore a different side of Ellis Native Alaskan roots. In North Carolina, Eustace Conway harvests the strongest wood to build new forges and upgrade Turtle Islands blacksmith shop.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Eustace hunts for his winter meat in the pre-dawn hours. Jake harvests a deer with bow and arrow. Tom helps his friend Paul prepare for trapping season. Ray stalks elk through knee deep powder, and spends a frigid night on the mountain. Aron hauls his winter supplies over a treacherous off-road trail, but the final stretch of the mission proves most daunting.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Tom instructs young Hank on some essential Mountain Man skills. Newlyweds Jake and Anika hunt elk in the bone chilling cold; Paul takes on an infestation of nuisance badgers; Ray uses bushcraft technology to haul his elk meat out of the backcountry; Aron and Jen clash with a pack of wolves prowling the outskirts of their homestead.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: On a winter deer hunt with Sean and Hank, Tom brings a classic muzzleloader. Jake and Anika finish their successful elk hunt with a grueling pack out. Aron and Jen trimmed down dead trees that were threatening their cabin.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Eustace and his neighbor are training a stubborn stallion. Jake and his dog team successfully chased away the seasons first mountain lion. Paul pursues and kills a juvenile deer. Aron goes moose hunting, while Jen goes rainbow trout fishing. Kidd and Harry track down cattle that have strayed into perilous territory.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Hank receives a buckskin vest from Tom and Nancy; Jakes snowmobile breaks down while hes at Lion Alley; Paul helps a neighbor with his beaver and muskrat issues; Kidd and Harry clear an airstrip in the backcountry.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Jake spends the night in the mountains after his snowmobile breaks down; Paul traps for mink and pine marten; Eustace helps a friend buy and assemble an antique sawmill; Aron and Jen harvest 12,000 pounds of ice from the lake.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Tom begins the countdown to his 80th birthday; Jake and Anika tow a broken snowmobile down the mountain but run into fresh lion tracks and are back on the hunt; Paul sets out to take on a wolf pack.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Kidd and Harry navigate turbulent whitewater to reach steelhead trout breeding grounds. Jake and his dogs become separated after following lion tracks into a remote and unfamiliar area.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Jake and his dogs tree three cats in one day. Paul prepares his haul of winter fur for market and explores a new piece of land to call his own. Eustace builds a boat lift using massive logs and a block and tackle pulley.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Tom builds a tent for his 80th birthday, while Nancy prepares a surprise party for him. During calving season, Jake runs a mountain lion off the road. Paul transports his trapping shed to new land across a steep and narrow mountain route. Kidd and Harry dig a root cellar into the side of a hill. Ray makes fish oil from freshly captured walleye to use in his traps.

programme.title: Alone
programme.desc: Take an exclusive peek behind the scenes in the last days leading up to the start of ALONE: Arctic Circle. As producers and participants make their final preparations and safety checks, the ten new survivalists are unveiled for the first time. With launch day looming, both the participants and the ALONE crew hustle to gear up for the most remote season yet.

programme.title: Alone
programme.desc: Ten new survivalists must survive north of the Arctic Circle as they face the cold and dwindling sun for a chance to win $500,000. While the participants prepare for frigid temperatures and dangerous predators, the Mackenzie River Delta begins to test each survivalist. With signs of grizzly bears and wolves all around, one survivalist learns the biggest danger could be himself.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: For centuries, fantastic tales of terrifying monsters have fascinated mankind. Bizarre beasts of all shapes and sizes have been portrayed in some of historys most classic stories and folklore. Examining what if these creatures were more than just works of imagination and superstition; if these stories of legendary creatures could be inspired by actual events and where does the line between fact and fiction fall; and if these famous monsters still lurk in the dark corner of the globe.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.Sub-title: The Bermuda Triangle and the Beyond
programme.desc: All over the world are forbidden triangles-mysterious regions where ships, planes, and people enter and then just disappear. They are found in Bermuda, Alaska, Japan and elsewhere. But why? Do these locations defy the laws of physics? Or is it possible that there are some places on Earth that we are simply not meant to go?

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: For centuries archaeologists and explorers have been obsessed with locating ancient cities lost to time. Some of these places are recorded in historical texts, while others are part of oral traditions and folklore. How does a once-thriving community become a lost city. And if their ruins are found, what secrets might be they reveal about human history.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: Various stories of mysterious disappearances are featured.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: What is it about rocks and stones that inspires some people to worship them. Throughout history, many have insisted that simple stones can do everything from choosing a king to causing death. A miraculous meteorite falling from the heavens. A priceless gemstone with a deadly curse. And a chunk of sandstone considered so sacred that wars have been fought to protect it.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: An examination of the prominence that gold wields over the collective consciousness.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Investigating if King Arthur was simply a thrilling figure cobbled together from the folklore of many cultures or a flesh and blood man. King Arthur. Englands first pop culture superhero, his exploits have inspired numerous blockbuster movies and television series. Without King Arthur, there wouldnt have "Middle Earth," "Harry Potter," "Game of Thrones," "Star Wars" or "Star Trek." It is impossible to gauge the impact that the Arthurian legend has had on Western culture.

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Thousands of people report looking up at the sky and witnessing unidentified flying objects. A vast majority of these strange and disturbing incidents take place in the same locations on Earth. Examining if it is possible that extraterrestrial spacecraft are repeatedly visiting Earth at so-called "UFO Hot Spots."

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: The animal kingdom is full of deadly predators, and some of them attack in extraordinary and inexplicable ways. Do some animals strike back at humans for reasons beyond normal defense and hunger? Can they have emotions like anger or a desire for revenge? Despite our technological advancement, do animals pose a greater threat to us than we realize for reasons that we cannot explain?

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: From massive asteroids and deadly bursts of radiation to microscopic alien viruses, each one of these cosmic dangers may end human civilization as we know it. Can we prepare for the inevitable calamity that may be coming our way?

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: The blue marble we call Earth remains in large part a mystery to the 8 billion people who live on it. How did Earth come to exist, and what are the secrets of its survival? How long will humans be able to call it "home"?

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Venus, our nearest planetary neighbor, is a glowing yellow orb with massive volcanoes, poisonous clouds, and a surface temperature hot enough to melt lead. Its appearance has fascinated us for centuries, but it raises questions about whether Venus is our evil twin or a warning about Earths future.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: As we aim to travel beyond the Moon to new worlds, how are we training to be human extraterrestrials? How will we survive this journey into the unknown? Will we be able to thrive when we reach our next galactic destination? And what will happen if we make contact with intelligent life?

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Discover what we could find when we reach other planets, and what dangers we may face.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Earths moon is only one of hundreds in the solar system, each of them a mysterious and often bizarre world. Examining if alien moons offer a key to exploring outer space and perhaps even finding a new home for humanity.

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Nearly eighty years after WWII, curious stories remain unexplained.

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: The Vikings may not be the barbarians theyve long been alleged to be, but a highly advanced civilization.

programme.title: The Cars That Made America
programme.desc: Henry Ford, the Dodge Brothers, William Durant and Walter Chrysler risk their livelihoods as they fight to bring a revolution in transportation to the masses. Their struggle laid the groundwork for some of the largest corporate empires in American history.

programme.title: The Cars That Made America
programme.desc: The history of American-made cars is covered, specifically those of the three dominating companies?Ford, General Motors, and Chrysler.

programme.title: The Cars That Made America
programme.desc: In the aftermath of WWII, America is moving to the suburbs, fueling a huge demand for new cars. Its a race to give new American consumers what they want, and the result is the golden age of the American automobile. The 50s and 60s youth want speed and fun, and the Big Three battle each other to give it to them, creating the all-American muscle car.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos, Erich von D niken, David Childress, William Henry, and Linda Moulton Howe are spotlighted.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.Sub-title: Ancient Aliens On Location: Evidence of Alien Life
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos looks back at the most incredible evidence of alien life ever uncovered.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In Ireland, the notion of the fairy folk dates back thousands of years and originates with stories of powerful shining beings who came down from the sky. Examining if these tales could have been inspired by ancient alien visitation. And if so, could the Shining Ones still inhabit the Irish mounds, existing in a parallel dimension.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Ancient Astronaut Theorists suggest obelisks of ancient Egypt may have served a special purpose, powering a technology that allowed for communication with the gods who came from the stars.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The Mutual UFO Network, better known as MUFON, is the worlds leading organization devoted to the scientific investigation of unidentified aerial phenomena and other unexplained encounters. With more than five decades of investigations, examining if MUFONs extensive database could contain definitive proof of extraterrestrial visitation.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Over 7,000 years ago, the ancient Sumerians established the worlds first civilization, and they credited their extraordinary achievements to their gods, the Anunnaki.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Across the globe, monumental stone figures silently bear witness to a forgotten era; on Easter Island, there are hundreds of Moai figures carved from volcanic rock, each weighing up to 90 tons, with the tallest standing over 30 feet.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos takes a look back at "The Top Ten Extraordinary Creatures" investigated ? creatures that just might provide compelling evidence of extraterrestrial visitation.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Counting down the top ten alien artifacts.

programme.desc: Kevin gets his first job at a burger joint; Fred is put to the test when she encounters a sinister shopkeeper.

programme.title: Hamster and Gretel
programme.Sub-title: When Life Gives You Lemons / Self- HEELP
programme.desc: Bailey and Gretel open a lemonade stand. Freds mom, Stacy, hosts a motivational speaking event.

programme.title: Kiff
programme.Sub-title: Thirst to be the First/The Fourth Bath
programme.desc: Kiff wants the first sip at a new water fountain and nothing will stop her. Then, Kiff learns that, at Barrys house, his family shares bathwater and Kiff is fourth in line.

programme.title: Kiff
programme.desc: Helen agrees to cast a swim spell on Barry if Kiff introduces her to Candles dad, Roy Fox; a road trip goes awry when scary-looking bikers pursue the Chatterleys and Barry.

programme.title: Kiff
programme.desc: Barry and Kiff investigate where Barrys brother goes when he skips class. They get summer jobs at City Hall and help the citizens of Table Town in any way they can.

programme.title: Kiff
programme.Sub-title: The Five Pigeons of the Acapellapocalypse/Leave a Little Juice
programme.desc: Kiff unleashes five forces of chaos, and Helen is the only one who can help.

programme.title: Kiff
programme.desc: A computer error moves Barry into high school, forcing Kiff to follow.

programme.title: Kiff
programme.desc: Kiff spontaneously creates the perfect mixture of cereals, she, Barry and Trevor try to recreate it; Kiff joins Pilot School to appear grown up to Beryl and her friends.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.Sub-title: A Hard Days Knight; I Brobot
programme.desc: Phineas, Ferb and Candace visit their grandparents in England and the boys create a renaissance fair, where Candace ends up in a jousting tournament; Doofenschmirtz attends a British convention dedicated to evil; the boys clone themselves so they can get more projects completed; Doofenschmirtz hovers over his girlfriends house with a huge magnet to erase crazy messages he recorded on her answering machine.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.Sub-title: Moms Birthday Episode; Journey to the Center of Candace
programme.desc: The boys plan a birthday surprise for their mum, complete with a special meal, musicians and a fashion show; Phineas and Ferb build a shrinking submarine so they can go inside Isabellas dog and retrieve a sash the pet ate, but they accidentally end up in Candaces stomach instead.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.Sub-title: Run Away Runway; I Scream, You Scream
programme.desc: The boys construct the biggest ice-cream maker for Isabella when her tonsils are taken out.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.Sub-title: Its a Mud, Mud, Mud, Mud World; The Ballad of Badbeard
programme.desc: The boys create a monster truck for Candace to practise driving, but soon her trial run turns into a massive racing rally; Doofenshmirtz tries to find investors for his line of monster trucks that are actual monsters; the boys set out on a high-seas adventure after learning about a mysterious pirate from their grandpa; Candace spies Perry and Dr Doofenschmirtz up to their old tricks.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.Sub-title: Dude, Were Gettin the Band Back Together!
programme.desc: The boys locate their parents favourite band, Love Handel, to perform a reunion concert in the backyard to make up for dad forgetting his wedding anniversary; Doofenshmirtz plans a birthday party for his daughter Vanessa.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.Sub-title: Ready for the Bettys; The Flying Fishmonger
programme.desc: Candace and Stacy win a day with their favourite rock band; the boys help Grandpa Fletcher return to his daredevil days by assisting him in a jump over a gorge.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.desc: The boys are sent to the countrys toughest reform school after their mum catches them making a flying car. Once there, an investigative reporter does a story on them, which prompts Candace to bust them out.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.Sub-title: Greece Lightning; Leave the Busting to Us!
programme.desc: The boys stage a chariot race through the towns streets after learning about ancient Greece; Candace uses a reality show to secretly film her brothers designing a whitewater rafting ride.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.Sub-title: Crack That Whip; The Best Lazy Day Ever
programme.desc: The boys build a roller-derby rink for their grandma, who once was on a championship derby team, so she can skate against her one-time rival; Doofenshmirtz believes a statue of a bearded man is mocking him because he cant grow facial hair; the boys decide to spend the day doing absolutely nothing, but Candace doesnt believe them and ends up constructing something of her own; Doofenshmirtz invents an ugly-inator so he will be more handsome than everyone else.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.Sub-title: Boyfriend From 27,000 B.C.; Voyage to the Bottom of Buford
programme.desc: The boys discover a caveman during an archaeological dig, and he falls in love with Candace, who is dressed like a cave girl for a costume party; Phineas and Ferb help Buford hunt for his missing goldfish.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.Sub-title: Does This Duckbill Make Me Look Fat?; Put That Putter Away
programme.desc: The boys construct a miniature golf course; Doofenshmirtz moves to the suburbs and gets annoyed by his neighbour; Phineas and Ferb invent a body transporter and it causes Candace and Perry to switch bodies.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.desc: Candace tries to get footage from a traffic camera to prove that her brothers create unusual stuff, but Perry wants to destroy the video to preserve his secret identity; the boys build the worlds largest bowling ball and pins; Doofenshmirtz tries to get rid of all rainbows.

programme.title: Bakugan
programme.Sub-title: For the Fate of the Misfit Clan; My Fingers Were Crossed!
programme.desc: Dan and Drago challenge Kage and Nillious to brawl for the fate of the Misfit Clan! Our heroes must convince the other clans to join them to save the city.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Sevens
programme.desc: Student Council President vetoes Yugas idea.

programme.title: Beyblade Burst QuadStrike
programme.Sub-title: Turbo Time! Zeal Achilles! / Super Z! Zest Achilles!
programme.desc: Aiger Akabane has arrived at Phantoms Gate.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.Sub-title: Ask a Foolish Question; Misperceived Monotreme
programme.desc: Phineas and Ferb create a computer that can answer any question, so Candace asks it to tell her how to bust her brothers; the guys build a fun house; and Perry misses his 100th battle against Doofenshmirtz when Candace takes him to a charity pet wash.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.desc: Phineas and Ferb make a phone for Candace with a transfer app that transports her to Easter Island; the boys create a magic carpet; Doofenshmirtz tries to ruin his brothers painting when its unveiled at an art show.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb
programme.desc: Phineas and Ferb create a hair-growing machine to correct Candaces bad haircut; Jamie Oliver judges the towns meatloaf festival cook-off and Doofenshmirtz enters his familys meatloaf recipe in the competition. Davy Jones and Peter Noone perform.
